name: Pokai Bay
tagline: The calmest water on the west side thanks to a protective breakwater — a true locals' family beach.
parking: Free lot at Pokai Bay Beach Park
bestTime: Year-round; the breakwater keeps the bay calm even when other west side beaches are rough
talkStory: Named after a legendary Polynesian voyager who sailed from this bay, Pokai Bay's breakwater creates the calmest protected swimming on the entire Waianae coast. On the rocky point sits Ku'ilioloa Heiau, one of the oldest navigational temples in Hawaii — ancient voyagers prayed here before setting out across the Pacific. Local families camp here on weekends with full setups, fishing and cooking all day. This is not a tourist beach; it is a living community gathering place, and the respect you give will be returned tenfold.
